spec.name: Web-Hung Path
spec.description: The path ahead is increasingly enclosed by webbing that hangs from both sides in heavy curtains, grey-white and thick enough to be opaque in places. Moving through requires pushing the silk aside, a task with an unpleasant texture and an equally unpleasant smell — something between old meat and metal. The webs have a remarkable tensile strength for their apparent delicacy, and there is a faint vibration in them, as though something distant is moving. Parcels of silk the size of a man's fist are suspended at intervals, wrapped tight around shapes that have ceased moving.
